audit: .work/insights/WORK-0022-install-guards-audit.md — both bugs REAL (cmd_install already guarded via WORK-0016; cmd_status/cmd_doctor crash; includes recursion). Decision: FIX (change_class stays fix).

cycle_1.historian: PASS — audit-first pattern followed; change_class fix correct; TiersIncludeCycleError mirrors ProjectionContainmentError; behaviour change (missing tier now errors) documented.
cycle_1.security-auditor: CONDITIONAL (MINOR) — no size cap; tier printed before validate; double flatten; deep-acyclic RecursionError; silent downgrade; tiers.json corruption (out of scope).
cycle_1.edge-case-hunter: CONDITIONAL (3 SIGNIFICANT) — {"tier":""} -> KeyError; unknown tier in includes -> KeyError; unknown installed tier -> KeyError in cmd_status/doctor. + diamond duplicate, assert-under-O MINORs.
cycle_1.simplicity-enforcer: CONDITIONAL (MINOR) — cycle-check duplicated across both flatten fns; asserts.

fixes: Unified _flatten_projections/_flatten_inits into _flatten_tier_key with cycle + unknown-tier + depth (_MAX_INCLUDE_DEPTH=32) guards under a TiersConfigError base (SIG-2, SIG-3, deep-recursion, dedup).
main() catches TiersConfigError (base) -> clean exit for cycle/unknown-tier/depth from any command.
cmd_install prints WARN on an unreadable marker before proceeding as fresh (no silent downgrade).
Tests: empty tier, non-dict top-level, unknown-tier reference, diamond-not-a-cycle, excessive depth, install-warns.

cycle_2.edge-case-hunter: PASS — all 3 SIGNIFICANT resolved; depth cap + diamond verified. New: 3 MINOR (inconsistent guard but main-caught; diamond duplicate now commented; unreachable main return 0).
cycle_2.security-auditor: CONDITIONAL PASS — in-scope MINORs resolved/justifiably deferred. New MINORs: _load_tiers corruption (out of scope, follow-up), print-before-validate (operator-only trust boundary).

deferred_out_of_scope: _load_tiers() raises a raw traceback on a corrupt/missing tiers.json (the bundled manifest). Same 'no traceback' spirit but beyond WORK-0022's scope (.hedl-tier + includes-cycle). Candidate follow-up item.
.hedl-tier file-size cap and ANSI-in-tier print: accepted — .hedl-tier is a per-install, git-ignored, operator-controlled file (self-inflicted, not a trust-boundary attack).
verdict: RESOLVED — zero BLOCKING/SIGNIFICANT after 2 cycles; residual MINOR/out-of-scope noted.
